[[Baron/Legends|Baron]] '''Ronnan Tyla Vedij''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] from a wealthy [[Vedij family|family]] who was part of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic's]] [[Republic Navy/Legends|navy]]. Vedij quickly obtained the [[Military rank/Legends|rank]] of [[Admiral/Legends|admiral]], and later retired on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] while his two sons, [[Dhas Fenoep Vedij]] and [[Elon Vedij]], served in the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], the Republic's successor state. In [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], Vedij was [[Death/Legends|killed]] by a traffic accident on Coruscant that Dhas believed was engineered by his [[Parent/Legends|father's]] enemies because the Baron had spoken out against the reworking of the Imperial [[Navy/Legends|Navy]].

==Biography==
Ronnan Tyla Vedij was a [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] into a position of considerable wealth, and had [[Vedij family|family]] holdings on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. A [[Baron/Legends|Baron]], he chose to join<ref name="Orbit" /> the [[Republic Navy/Legends|navy]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]],<ref name="Note" /> and, unlike his ancestors, took a [[Space warfare/Legends|combat]]-related role instead of buying a safe position. Vedij led a distinguished career in the [[Navy/Legends|navy]], and once, when he was a [[Bosun/Legends|bosun]], [[Unidentified battle (Ronnan Tyla Vedij)|suffered]] heavy wounds at the hands of [[Alien/Legends|alien]] [[Pirate/Legends|commerce raiders]]. Vedij managed to deter the assault by himself, and this event caused him to be promoted to the [[Military rank/Legends|rank]] of [[Captain/Legends|captain]]. Vedij later became an [[Admiral/Legends|admiral]], and while he was still in the navy, had two sons, [[Dhas Fenoep Vedij|Dhas]] and [[Elon Vedij]], with his [[Ronnan Tyla Vedij's wife|wife]].<ref name="Orbit" />

The Baron Admiral retired to Coruscant, and Vedij brought his sons up to believe in a strong military, something that was also believed by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], the Republic's successor state. Dhas later joined the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]], and was followed some [[Standard year/Legends|years]] later by Elon. When Imperial [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] began to retool the [[Imperial Military/Legends|military]], Vedij became a vocal opponent of this movement. In [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], shortly after Dhas was transferred to the [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate]] ''[[Far Orbit]]'', Vedij and his [[Marriage/Legends|wife]] were [[Death/Legends|killed]] on Coruscant in a traffic accident, following a [[Metrocab Robo-Hack Landspeeder|Robo-Hack]] misinterpreting routing instructions which caused it to go in the opposite direction. After Dhas, who inquired into the nature of the crash, was warned away by the [[Imperial Security Bureau/Legends|Imperial Security Bureau]], he believed that his [[Parent/Legends|father]] had been killed by the enemies he had made by opposing the retooling of the military. Dhas later led a [[Mutiny on the Far Orbit|mutiny]] on the ''Far Orbit'' because of the deaths of both his father and brother, who died [[Battle of the Silent Blades|fighting]] pirates shortly after Vedij.<ref name="Orbit" />

==Personality and traits==
A man who believed in a strong military, Vedij chose to enter the line of combat in the navy instead of buying a safe position. Vedij was also a vocal opponent of Palpatine's changes to the military of the Empire.<ref name="Orbit" />

==Behind the scenes==
Ronnan Tyla Vedij was first mentioned in [[1998]]'s ''[[The Far Orbit Project]]'', by [[Timothy S. O'Brien]]. The book was a supplement to [[West End Games]]'s ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' and was a sequel of sorts to the previous publication ''[[Pirates & Privateers]]'', carrying on that book's theme by following the [[Privateer/Legends|privateering]] [[Starship/Legends|vessel]] ''Far Orbit''.<ref name="Orbit" />
